title: MANGA This is a mistake right
display_title: MANGA This is a mistake right
wikitext:
[]: This is a mistake, right?             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sat Oct 29, 2005 6:39 am So, I was reading volume8 of the manga before, and I noticed something incredibly odd. Now, I know Sadamato has made some big-ass changes in the manga, but please tell me that putting Yui's soul inside Zerogouki was not one of them, and it was just a mistake. I mean, that would be just silly, right?

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Sat Oct 29, 2005 8:24 am I think that was a mistake. Or, he was just trying to change up some things to what he believes happened in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Oct 29, 2005 8:57 am Where's Carl Horn with the official line on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 29, 2005 11:46 am Well, on my first viewing, I got the impression that it was Unit-00 that had been involved in the first contact experiment, so I can see where he might be coming from. And given his complete rewriting of both Rei and Asuka (heck, all the Children), I wouldn't be surprised if this was a deliberate, not an accident.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Sat Oct 29, 2005 11:59 pm Sadamoto probably viewed the series only once.

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Sun Oct 30, 2005 1:13 am Sadamoto probably viewed the series only once.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sun Oct 30, 2005 1:22 am he's joking of course :P :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Sun Oct 30, 2005 1:54 am Six Winged Angel wrote: he's joking of course :P :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun Oct 30, 2005 2:36 am http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=36

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sun Oct 30, 2005 2:45 am the_seventh_child wrote: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=36

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Oct 30, 2005 10:04 am Phoenix Feet wrote: And for the record, he hasn't totally re-written Rei or Asuka or any of the other characters. The differences are surface ones only.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Sun Oct 30, 2005 9:13 pm Phoenix Feet wrote: Sadamoto probably viewed the series only once.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Oct 31, 2005 4:10 pm To mention the specific Eva Unit was a mistake, just like the omission of the entry for "Adam" in the dossier section of the unflopped books. I've asked that these be corrected in future printings. This reminds me of a 16th-century printing of the Bible that became known as the "Wicked Bible," because a typo left out the "not" in "thou shalt not commit adultery." For a while people were going around waving it to people of the opposite sex in their neighborhood, saying, "But it's in the BIBLE!"

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Mon Oct 31, 2005 4:21 pm Whoa. I never heard about that. I learned something today...

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Nov 07, 2005 5:49 am God, I feel so friggin' ignorant around here >.> Seriously, I am starting to realize how little I know compared to certain individuals and the collective flock here. And here I was thinking that I was ready for Jeparody... I'd never have put Unit 01 as being made in 2004... Because that would mean they were running both proto-types (Units 00 and 01) at the same time... Unless they lazily slapped together Unit 00. Then again, as far as I know, Unit 01 is Yui... I think I am reading into this too much. Meh. I have some ideas running through my head, but they'd probably be shot down fairly quickly and many don't pretain to the topic at hand. Maybe Gendo just put trap doors and Yui stepped on one, disappearing until later.

Now, I know Sadamato has made some big-ass changes in the manga, but please tell me that putting Yui's soul inside Zerogouki was not one of them, and it was just a mistake. I mean, that would be just silly, right?
